rules.description: Produce the most natural sounding translation, capturing intended meaning and transferring it into natural language suited to a wide French-speaking audience.
rules.notes: Avoid literal transposition of English syntax in French; sentences often need to be rewritten with adapted syntax.

rules.description: Use a clear and precise style. Translation should be easily understood and not read like a translation.
rules.notes: Balance faithfulness to source and readability in target language.

rules.description: Avoid heaviness, ambiguities, repetitions and Anglicism. Also avoid enriching the text excessively.
Follow priority order: 1. DNT list 2. Style guide 3. Glossary 4. Translation memory
rules.notes: Client's specific instructions should take precedence over this document if there are conflicts.

rules.description: If software has been translated, match the relevant translation in the resources provided.
If software has not been translated, use bilingual format: English first, followed by target in brackets.
rules.example_correct: Cliquez sur Open File (Ouvrir un fichier).

rules.description: UI options should typically be translated as verbs, generally using the infinitive form. Be concise, avoid articles or prepositions and go straight to the point.
rules.notes: Write the first letter of the option with an uppercase.

rules.description: For compound UI options, be clear and direct.
Use 'et' instead of '&' in UI options and avoid literal translations.

rules.section: Names of RingCentral Products, Applications and Features
rules.description: Check reference material and linguistic instructions, including terminology and DNT lists, before translating any product, application, third party or feature names.
rules.notes: Many product/application names are trademarked and not localizable.

rules.description: Replace English examples with French equivalents when needed for localization/adaptation.
rules.example_correct: searching on 'chasse' will select any knowledge base items that contain 'chasse', 'pêche', 'sonner'
rules.example_incorrect: searching on '*ing' will select any knowledge base items that contain 'fishing', 'hunting', 'ring'
rules.notes: Good example is wildcard operator explanations that need French word examples.

rules.description: Understand who the audience is (end users, partners, etc.) before translating.
Be precise and clear, consistent and natural. Rephrase sentences that are not clear enough, and rework sentences that sound like a translation.
rules.notes: Break up long English sentences into smaller friendlier parts for better understanding.

rules.description: Use accurate and concise style with no ambiguities or mistranslations.
Be clear and coherent so target reader should understand the text easily.
Be objective: neutral, do not insert your own opinion, avoid colloquial or slang words, be always politically correct.
Be correct: content and numbers should be free of mistakes.
Be idiomatically correct: use correct verbs, metaphors, expressions so text does not sound like a translation.
Be complete: do not leave out content (words, phrases, whole paragraphs).
Keep an eye on the target reader: use an appropriate tone.
Be consistent: use same structure and translate the same way between two files, even if they are different (i.e operating systems).
For internal cross-references, translate references to chapters, sections, headings or titles within the document consistently.
rules.notes: Internal cross-references mention other parts of text within the file or document being translated.

rules.description: For external cross-references, check if text or document has been localized before and match relevant translations.
rules.notes: External cross-references are to titles of other documents not being translated or already translated in previous project.

rules.description: If external document has not been translated before, leave as in source language.
rules.notes: Contact Project Management team or French Language Lead if unable to retrieve translation information.

rules.description: Do not overuse 'S'il vous plaît'. Be more direct and use imperative form or similar constructions where appropriate.
rules.example_incorrect: S'il vous plaît, entrez votre nom d'utilisateur.
rules.notes: French does not often use the equivalent of English 'Please'.

rules.description: French usually gives priority to masculine forms, unless differently needed based on specific context.
rules.example_correct: Le service contactera le client pour l'informer.
rules.example_incorrect: Le service contactera le client/la cliente pour l'informer.

rules.description: Use workaround to avoid gender issues before country tags.
rules.example_correct: Les tarifs sortants locaux s'appliquent lors des appels émis vers le pays suivant : {CountryName}
rules.example_incorrect: Les tarifs sortants locaux s'appliquent lors des appels émis vers le/la/les/l'/{CountryName}

rules.description: Avoid passive voice whenever possible, which is too formal and wordy. Use active form to improve readability.
rules.example_correct: Le logiciel sauvegarde tous les fichiers dans le répertoire.
rules.example_incorrect: Tous les fichiers sont sauvegardés dans le répertoire par le logiciel.
rules.notes: Passive voice is only advised in specific texts like legal and official documents.

rules.description: In presentation contexts, use active voice for better readability.
rules.example_correct: Ce livre blanc présente certaines fonctionnalités nouvelles.
rules.example_incorrect: Certaines fonctionnalités nouvelles sont présentées dans ce livre blanc.

rules.description: Do not transfer extensive use of English possessive adjectives to French. Simple articles can often replace possessive adjectives.
rules.example_correct: Vous pouvez éditer les fichiers sur un ordinateur ou appareil mobile.
rules.example_incorrect: Vous pouvez éditer vos fichiers sur votre ordinateur ou votre appareil mobile.
rules.notes: In some cases, 'your' refers to a subject in third person.

rules.description: For proper use of articles with foreign words, follow standard French grammar rules.
rules.notes: Examples include: (la Süddeutsche Zeitung), (les Big Data)

rules.description: The –ing form can be translated in different ways based on context: as a verb, noun, infinitive clause, subordinate clause, gerund etc. Use the most appropriate option.
rules.example_correct: Nos contrôleurs sont livrés avec une mémoire cache qui protège l'intégrité de vos données
rules.notes: Choose among various translation options based on context.

rules.description: English sometimes uses additional words to express concepts. Check context, concentrate on meaning and render it as clearly as possible.
rules.example_correct: Nom, adresse électronique, nom de société
rules.example_incorrect: Votre nom, votre adresse électronique, votre nom de société

rules.description: Simplify transitional expressions.
rules.example_incorrect: Ceci signifie que vous pouvez désormais...

rules.description: Repetitions are acceptable in English but not in French. Use pronouns or other techniques to avoid them.
rules.example_correct: Pour supprimer un fichier sur votre ordinateur, sélectionnez-le dans le volet de droite, puis cliquez sur Supprimer...
rules.example_incorrect: Pour supprimer un fichier sur votre ordinateur, sélectionnez le fichier dans le volet de droite, puis cliquez sur Supprimer...

rules.description: Be careful with false friends and literal translations that can have different meaning from source.
rules.notes: For English 'actually'

rules.description: Translate 'support' correctly based on context.
Translate 'comprehensive' correctly.
Translate 'demand' correctly.
Translate 'issue' correctly.
Translate 'surname' correctly.
Translate 'pass exams' correctly.
Translate 'balance' correctly.
Use capital letters for proper names, product names, geographical names.
Use capital letters for the first word after a period, exclamation mark, question mark, ellipsis.
In title case, use capitals for nouns and adjectives of organizations, bodies, departments, awards, and events.
Use lowercase for nationalities, languages, religions, days of the week, months, season names, currencies, job titles in French.
Use lowercase for months.
Use lowercase for currencies.
Use lowercase for languages.
Use lowercase for nationalities.
In titles, headings, UI options, only the first word of the string should be capitalized.
Only capitalize first word in page layout terms.
Only capitalize first word in model names.
In legal texts, retain capitalization that conveys specific meaning.
rules.example_correct: Le présent accord (l'« Accord ») est un accord contraignant
rules.example_incorrect: Le présent accord (l'« accord ») est un accord contraignant
rules.notes: Exception to general capitalization rules for legal implications.

rules.description: In legal software terms, capitalize appropriately.
rules.example_correct: Installez et utilisez une copie du Logiciel.
rules.example_incorrect: Installez et utilisez une copie du logiciel.

rules.description: Preserve all upper case formatting when whole paragraphs are written this way, as these conventions may have legal implications.
Run spell-checker when translation is completed.
Brand name tags need to be separated from preceding or following text with a space.
Use non-breaking space before double punctuation signs: colons, semicolons, question marks, exclamation marks and quotation marks.
rules.notes: Press Ctrl + Shift + Space bar in MS Word or use Alt+0160.

rules.description: Use non-breaking spaces to prevent certain expressions from being separated at end of line: people's names, numbers, software names and versions, etc.
Replace double spaces after full stops with single spaces in French.
rules.notes: English documents often include double spaces after full stops.

rules.description: No space is needed between a word and a round bracket.
Insert comma before 'et' only when absolutely required and at end of enumeration to avoid ambiguity.
A comma may be needed before 'et', 'ou' or 'ni' if coordinated elements have different subjects.
No comma is needed when coordinates are simple.
Insert comma between elements coordinated by conjunctions other than 'et', 'ou' or 'ni'.
Do not add comma before subordinate clauses.
rules.example_correct: Le service clientèle m'a informé que j'avais droit à une remise sur mon forfait téléphonique.
rules.example_incorrect: Le service clientèle m'a informé, que j'avais droit à une remise sur mon forfait téléphonique.
rules.notes: Common mistake originated by some CAT tools.

rules.description: Use period to end a sentence, paragraph or abbreviated word.
When sentence is placed between brackets or quotes, place period outside.
Do not use full stops after headings and titles, after columns in a table, or after captions used with illustrations, tables etc., unless the expression is a full sentence.
Do not use full stops if sentence ends with abbreviation, interrogation or exclamation mark, ellipsis, or colon.
Try to avoid using colons, as English makes wider use of them. Prefer commas or periods wherever possible.
rules.example_correct: Ouvrez l'assistant Créer un module et cliquez sur l'onglet Module.
rules.example_incorrect: Ouvrez l'assistant Créer un module : cliquez sur l'onglet Module.
rules.notes: Colon must be preceded by non-breaking space.

rules.description: In category descriptions, avoid colons and rewrite clearly.
rules.example_correct: La colonne Catégorie contient deux entrées : Statut et Configuration. OU : La colonne Catégorie contient les entrées Statut et Configuration.
rules.example_incorrect: La colonne Catégorie contient les deux : Statut et Configuration.

rules.description: In examples, use colon appropriately.
Use semi-colons less than in English. Mainly use to separate items in enumeration when using only commas would be confusing. As a rule, avoid semi-colons and start new sentence instead.
rules.example_correct: Les catégories incluent les ordinateurs, ordinateurs portables et appareils mobiles, les systèmes d'exploitation, applications et applications mobiles, ainsi que les réseaux, routeurs et pare-feu.
rules.example_incorrect: Les catégories incluent : les ordinateurs, ordinateurs portables et appareils mobiles ; les systèmes d'exploitation, applications et applications mobiles ; les réseaux, routeurs et pare-feu.
rules.notes: Semi-colon must be preceded by non-breaking space.

rules.description: Replace semi-colons with periods to create separate sentences for better readability.
rules.example_correct: Notez les informations contenues dans les champs Nom d'utilisateur et Mot de passe, vous en aurez besoin plus tard pour vous connecter. OU : Notez les informations contenues dans les champs Nom d'utilisateur et Mot de passe. Vous en aurez besoin plus tard pour vous connecter.
rules.example_incorrect: Notez les informations contenues dans les champs Nom d'utilisateur et Mot de passe ; vous en aurez besoin plus tard pour vous connecter.

rules.description: Avoid question marks as they are much less used in French than in English.
rules.notes: Question mark must be preceded by non-breaking space. For headings.

rules.description: Replace questions with descriptive headings.
Exclamation marks are not as common in French as in English. Sometimes it is better to remove them.
rules.notes: Exclamation mark must be preceded by non-breaking space.

rules.description: When exclamation marks appear in groups (!!!), keep only one.
Use hyphen/en dash in compound terms and for page ranges. Don't use en dash as minus sign, use minus symbol instead.
rules.notes: Use spaces around minus symbol.

rules.description: Use colon or comma to replace en dash in product names.
Replace en dash with 'à' for indicating ranges.
rules.example_correct: Du lun. Au ven. de 8 h à 16 h, Pages 1 à 4

rules.description: Use colon rather than dash for explanations.
rules.example_correct: Activer : cliquez sur cette option pour activer le serveur.
rules.example_incorrect: Activer — Cliquez sur cette option pour activer le serveur.

rules.description: Use round brackets for further explanations which would otherwise disrupt the sentence, for spelling out acronyms, for indicating singular/plural forms or for translations of UI items in cases where UI has not been localized.
rules.example_correct: Indiquez l'adresse (UNC ou URL), puis cliquez sur Valider.

rules.description: Use round brackets for spelling out acronyms.
rules.example_correct: Les fichiers volumineux peuvent être transférés par FTP (File Transfer Protocol, ou protocole de transfert de données).

rules.description: Use round brackets for indicating singular/plural forms.
Use square brackets for indicating variables.
rules.example_correct: L'adresse du serveur est spécifiée suivant le format \[Serveur]\[Domaine].

rules.description: Use square brackets for additional information within round brackets.
rules.example_correct: Indiquez l'adresse (adresse UNC [interne] ou adresse URL [externe]), puis cliquez sur Valider.

rules.description: NEVER use straight single quote for variables.
rules.notes: Updated on 20241207

rules.description: Use double quotes in French. Single quotes should be avoided unless software requirements or other constraints force their use.
Place punctuation outside the quotes.
Use angle quotation marks (guillemets). A non-breaking space must follow opening mark (« ) and precede closing mark ( »).
Use curly apostrophe as per FR convention.
Escape apostrophes before tags like this: '/{1}
Curly apostrophes do not need to be escaped before a tag, only straight ones as they can break the code. If source escapes apostrophes, there is no need to do it in FR, use curly apostrophes.

rules.section: Style to use in bulleted lists
rules.description: If bulleted items are complete sentences (with conjugated verb), each begins with capital and ends with period.
rules.notes: Example 1 – complete sentences

rules.section: Style to use in bulleted lists
rules.description: If bulleted items are phrases or single words, each begins with capital letter and shall not be followed by period.
rules.notes: Example 2 – phrases/words

rules.section: Style to use in bulleted lists
rules.description: If list items are not full sentences and depend syntactically on introductory sentence, each element should start with lowercase letter and end with semicolon. End last item with period.
rules.notes: Example 3 – continued sentences

rules.description: Abbreviations should be avoided whenever possible, but may be needed in UI options.
Shorten word by at least two letters, abbreviation should end with consonant and end with period.
If word has double consonant, abbreviate after the second consonant.
Translation of acronyms depends on RingCentral project-related requirements and should always be investigated to confirm their localizability.
If acronym does not have common translation and is before spelled-out version, keep English acronym.
If acronym has common translation and is before spelled-out version, use translated version between brackets.
If spelled-out term is followed by acronym between brackets and has common translation, replace English spelled-out version with translated version.
If there is no commonly used spelled-out translation, keep spelled-out term in English between brackets.
If there is commonly used translated acronym and spelled-out translation, insert translated version and acronym between brackets.
If acronym is not spelled out and has no common translated equivalent, keep acronym in English only.
If acronym is not spelled out and has common translated equivalent, use translated acronym only.
Write numbers in full when used as nouns.
Write numbers in full when at beginning of sentence.
Write numbers in full when appearing alone and representing simple quantities, usually numbers up to 9.
Write numbers in full in expressions like 'in the 80s'.
Write dates, large numbers, ages, document references (paragraph, page, etc.) as numbers.
Replace decimal point with comma and thousand separator with dot.
Format large numbers with dots as thousand separators and commas as decimal separators.
Format numbers consistently with French conventions.
Format large numbers with spaces as thousand separators.
Avoid mixing numerical and alphabetical forms.
Units of measure should be separated from preceding number by non-breaking space.
For imperial system units not applying to French, either add converted value first with original in brackets, or convert to metric equivalent based on project requirements.
rules.notes: Monitor sizes are always expressed in inches and represent exception.

rules.description: Write percentages in figures followed by % symbol. Use non-breaking space between figure and symbol.
Adapt date and time formats to French standards. Date format should follow day-month, not month-day.
Use short date format DD/MM/YYYY separated by slash, less frequently by hyphen.
Use long date format dddd MMMM yyyy.
Use standard time format hh:mm:ss with 24-hour clock format. Use non-breaking space in dates and times.
Use French time range format.
Use French date format for extended dates.
Convert ISO date format to French format.
Place currency symbols after numeral and preceded by non-breaking space, with two decimal figures.
To distinguish between US dollars and CA dollars, use ISO abbreviation in parentheses.
An ellipsis indicates omitted words in direct quote—not a thought that's trailing off.
Don't capitalize first word after ellipsis (unless it's a proper noun).
Don't begin a sentence with an ellipsis.
Don't use spaces before or after an ellipsis.
If ending a complete sentence with ellipsis, no period is needed.
An ellipsis is one character, not three periods. However, if ellipsis is needed in plain-text format, use three periods.
rules.example_correct: Four score and seven years... a new nation.
rules.notes: To create ellipsis on Mac: opt + [ ; ]; in Windows: hold alt and type 0133.

rules.description: Translate 'about' as 'à propos de'.
